data.body:
Lottery medical care kills as often as insurance company death panels.

Neither one is preferable, but the "monstrous" system offers ways around the lottery, which to me is a form of socialized care: your social group pays for your care.

Having no options if you lose the lottery seems cruel.

data.body: Back in 1999, Cliff Stoll wrote a book called "High Tech Heretic: Why Computers Don't Belong in the Classroom" and got roundly savaged for it.
